behavior_summary: Implements the NPC Vardion's interaction/dialogue state machine invoked when a player interacts with him.
On first contact (checked by global flag vardionMet) Vardion asks who the player is, obtains the player's name via getName(), and builds a menu of reply options presented with process::ask.

The player's chosen reply (returned string) is compared against multiple known responses; matching branches produce appropriate dialogue via process::bark, update NPC state flags (e.g., vardionAvaName) and anger level (vardionMad), and in at least one antagonistic branch spawn a separate process (spawn FREE::1AD0).

A small local interaction_state flag controls whether the NPC insists on an answer or repeats/prompts; local slist holds the menu, resp_str holds the selected response, and playerName holds the constructed 'I am <name>.' string used for identity comparisons.
notable_control_flow: Entry: U8P_VARDION2::0080 checks global vardionMet to select first-meeting vs repeat dialogue paths.
If first meeting: getName() is called and 'I am <name>.' is constructed and appended to the slist of reply options.
process::ask is called with slist; the returned string (resp_str) is strcmp'd against many canonical replies to determine branching.

Certain branches set vardionAvaName (when player claims to be the Avatar), others set/increment vardionMad (angering Vardion), and one branch spawns FREE::1AD0 (likely to escalate to assistance/hostility).
Looping/reprompt behavior is controlled by a local interaction_state which can force re-ask or proceed with handling the chosen response.
open_questions: Precisely what behavior or entity spawn FREE::1AD0 creates (summon, guard, or immediate combat) — inferred but not explicit.
Exact semantics and numeric meanings of vardionMad values (1,2,3) and how they influence future interactions beyond the immediate bark/branch.
Where and how vardionMet is set elsewhere in game scripts (outside this excerpt) and how repeat interactions differ in complete context.
The precise effect of interaction_state variations (exact conditions under which prompts are repeated vs accepted).

main_function.name: U8P_VARDION2::0080
main_function.purpose: Handle player interaction/dialogue with the NPC Vardion: ask identity, present reply options, evaluate player replies, emit dialogue, and update NPC state (recognition of Avatar, anger) with possible escalation (spawn).
entry_conditions: Triggered by player interaction with Vardion (e.g., conversation/trigger area entry).
Checks global vardionMet to determine first-time vs repeat behavior.

function_map.name: process::bark
function_map.role: Emit dialogue text from the NPC to the player

function_map.name: process::ask
function_map.role: Present a selectable list of player reply strings and return the chosen string

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION2::getName
function_map.role: Retrieve the player's name used to form identity reply comparisons

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1AD0
function_map.role: Spawn an external process/entity (likely assistance or hostile escalation)

external_calls.purpose: Output Vardion's dialogue lines in response to the player's choices or script-driven prompts.
Present the assembled slist of reply options to the player and retrieve the selected reply string for strcmp-driven branching.
Obtain the player's name so the script can create and compare an 'I am <name>.' reply to detect identity claims.
Spawn a separate process/entity as a side effect of an antagonistic player reply (likely to call for help or initiate hostility); exact spawn purpose is inferred.

reads.name: vardionMet
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether this is the first meeting path (affects whether identity/name prompt is used).

writes.name: vardionAvaName
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player claims to be the Avatar so Vardion records recognition of the Avatar identity.

writes.name: vardionMad
writes.likely_effect: Updated/incremented to track Vardion's anger/hostility level in response to antagonistic player replies; influences subsequent dialogue and actions.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the NPC Vardion's interaction/dialogue state machine invoked when a player interacts with him.
